slides.title: MAPRG_Towards a Common Measurement Framework for Large-Scale libp2p Deployments using CBOR-based Verifiable Telemetry _Johanna Moran_Manu Gupta
Measuring OAuth in MCP: Migration from DCR to CIMD
Feeding the Crawlers - An Experiment in Deliberate Bot Attraction
(Updated) A Browser's Perspective: Surprises and Insights from Firefox Networking Telemetry
Measuring Deployment Characteristics of Post-Quantum TLS Authentication Mechanisms
Understanding DNS Performance over the Starlink Network
The Future of DNS Privacy: A Comparison of DNS over QUIC and DNS over HTTP/3
Detecting and Characterizing Exposed BGP Routers
Noisy Routers: Investigating the Make-Up of Route Collector Data

chat.text: Johannes, have seen any evidence of TTL rewrites in protocols other than ICMP?

chat.text: sorry, other than UDP?

chat.text: Yes, we also tested icmp echo messages to check that it is independent from very small TTL values in the probes and saw it for TCP based traceroutes as well

chat.text:
Robert's paper is here, btw: https://robert-richter.me/pdfs/starlink-dns-dynamics.pdf
Also linked in the MAPRG agenda, in the abstracts section.

chat.author: Valentin Goșu
chat.text: @Robert Richter For DoE (and even TCP) measurements, it would be interesting if you also measured latency over a persistent connection (without the initial handshake delay)

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text: The paper Newton is presenting can be found here, unencumbered by paywall: https://vaibhavbajpai.com/documents/papers/proceedings/2026-pam-doq-doh3.pdf

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: remind me: is DoQ with 0-RTT vulnerable to replay if sent to an anycast instance?

chat.author: Benjamin Schwartz
chat.text: @stephen Complicated.  The answer is something like SHOULD NOT.  But the response is encrypted so you don't learn much from the replay.

chat.author: Jason Livingood
chat.text: those RTTs still seem high

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: unless you can also see the other side of the anycast instance

chat.author: Jason Livingood
chat.text: Oof - these PLTs show how bloated the avg site has gotten

chat.author: Erik Nygren
chat.text: @stephen: right, if you can see both sides you might be able to extract some things if the resolver is using Do53 to the authorities, but I suspect the correlation you can already do in that position is dominant even without 0RTT replay.  Since these are mostly idempotent (other than for the case you raise) it's unclear how much of a problem it is in practice.

chat.author: Lucas Pardue
chat.text: https://httparchive.org/reports/page-weight

chat.author: Jason Livingood
chat.text: aka "the web is so bloated, users won't notice slow DNS QRTs" ;-)

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: IIRC anycast DNS was the canonical example of when to not do 0-RTT

chat.author: Marco Munizaga
chat.text: Very different numbers of page weight from httparchive and the presenter's slides (1.2 mb vs 3mb)?

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text: We're going to close the queue after Erik Nygren.

chat.author: Benjamin Schwartz
chat.text: @Stephen In TLS 1.3, servers SHOULD prevent 0-RTT replay.  For large anycast networks, this is presumably done by partitioning resumption for each local server pool, and having a replay prevention database in each location.

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: Forgot to mention on the mic that for us, DoH resolves FASTER than Do53

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: At - I think - P75

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: @ben: have any of 'em siad that? if not, I'd be concerned if stubs were using 0-RTT (not emergency concerned but concerned)

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: mind you, I've been concerned about 0-RR from the start but the sky hasn't fallen yet:-)

chat.author: Benjamin Schwartz
chat.text: I believe this is a common practice in general for TLS.  Globally resumable session tickets require globally distributed secret keys, which is a very weak security posture.

chat.author: Jason Livingood
chat.text: For the presenter (since queue is closed) - when as an operator we optimize for RTT and delay in general, always look at p95 or p99 not mean or median as those will mislead and sometimes give the impression all is well. So p95 or p99.

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: https://performance.mozilla.org/networking.html has TRR DoH performance numbers

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: @lars: does FF use 0-RTT for DoH?

chat.author: Tim Chown
chat.text: I was just going to suggest he speaks to the web tester team about happy-eyeballs.net.  they're in Germany too I recall.

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text: Shyam's paper is available here, from TMA 2026: https://ris.utwente.nl/ws/portalfiles/portal/538780291/Exposed_BGP_routers.pdf

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text: good idea, @tim.

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: @stephen: Yes, but also the connection is very long lived

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: ack

chat.author: Lucas Pardue
chat.text: should DNS over HTTPS be using the QUERY method rther than POST?

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: We recently updated https://wiki.mozilla.org/Security/DOH-resolver-policy and one change was to require idle timeouts > 180s

chat.author: Lars Eggert
chat.text: @lucas Moz switched to GET recently

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text: A paper with the work Ebrima is reporting on is here: https://dl.ifip.org/db/conf/cnsm/cnsm2025/1571167978.pdf

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text:
Also, a shorter blog version here: 
https://blog.apnic.net/2026/04/16/noisy-routers-investigating-the-make-up-of-route-collector-data/

chat.author: Dave Plonka
chat.text:
For those that might be interested, the voluminous RouteViews data is mirrored to here: https://osg-htc.org/services/osdf/data?repository=routeviews
OSDF and partners are looking for tutorials/examples of projects using it via OSDF, or are interested in streaming access. Please let me know if you are!

chat.author: Manu Gupta
chat.text: Thanks for sharing, Dave. This looks quite relevant for the libp2p/IPFS ecosystem. The RouteViews dataset could be valuable for correlating Internet routing dynamics with P2P connectivity, DHT behavior, and network resilience. The streaming access is especially interesting for observability and measurement research. We'd be happy to learn more and explore potential collaboration.

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text: Please feel free to use the mailing list for further discussion/questions!

chat.author: Mirja Kühlewind
chat.text: (maprg@ietf.org)

chat.text: Great, thanks Mirja :)

chat.text: I'm suprised .jp domains are so low in IPv6 adoption?

chat.author: Manu Gupta
